Mytilus edulis, Littorina menae, Balanus crenatus and Bowerbankia imbricata all frequent, and one patch c. 1m2 of vertical bedrock had c. 200 limpets, and large barnacle cover. column2: Wide midshore band dominated by Ascophyllum on broken bedrock with many steep and overhanging surfacse. Fucus serratus and F. vesiculosus occasional. F. vesiculosus was the only fucoid on a large metal mooring loop. A small variety of red and green algae were recorded. Dynamena pumila and Laomedea flexuosa were super abundant on large brown algae. Semibalanus balanoides were common and Elminius modestus frequent; Patella vulgata and Littorina mariae common. column2: Upper shore bedrock overhung by oakwood with Woodrush, Honeysuckle, Holly and ferns etc. Rock surfaces with green and grey lichens, no yellow lichens present in the shadiest places, being replaced by green powdery alga/lichen, and Enteromorpha. Below this dense Pelvetia/Bostrychia and Fucus spiralis/ Catenella bands, with Audouinella bands locally abundant in patches on bedrock and stones. Anurida, Petrobius, red mites and Littorina saxitilis were recorded. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

Of the animals Hemimycale columella, Haliclona viscosa, Caryophyllia smithii, Marthasterias glacialis, Echinus esculentus and Labrus bergylta were likewise frequent in their abundance. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

column3: Two cores taken here at 2.5m ACD; 0.1m2/1mm mesh and 0.01m2/0.5mm mesh. Anoxic silty mud. Black layer at shallow depth (around 5cm). Small numbers of whole shells cover the smooth surface (largely Scrobicularia plana). The sediment is glutinous with deep sink. Large numbers of Arenicola casts were visible and bait digging activities nearby indicated the presence of large indigenous populations. Macrofaunal content of cores was polycheate dominated, - mainly Hediste diversicolor which was superabundant. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

column2: Overhangs and vertical rock at 0.8 to 1.8m ACD. Dominated by red algal turf, predominently Lomentaria articulata, Ceramium flabelligerum and Gigartina stellata. Animals included common encrusting bryozoans (Flustrellidra hispida, Electra pilosa), a few sponges (frequent Halichondria panicea and occasional Hymeniacidon perleve) Frequent Pomatoceros sp. and Dynamena pumila. column3: Lower infralittoral broken bedrock at 2.7 to 4.2m BCD. Thick sticky mud in hollows, and muddy rock surfaces. Fairly wide variety of scattered algae, including Stenogramme interrupta unusually on bedrock. Laminaria saccharina was rare. There was no L. hyperborea. Several characteristic silt tolerent species (Phyllophora crispa, P. pseudoceranoides, Cordylecladia erecta). Laminaria saccharina fronds loaded with Celleporella sp. Several fish - Goldsinnys and Scorpion fish. Many large sponges on ridges. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

column2: Coarse sandy mud with gravel, strongly rippled surfacte with very shallow sink. Surface cover consisted of some whole shells mainly Scrobicularia and some Enteromorpha attached to small pebbles and stones on the surface. (Empty shells of Mya arenaria and Cerastoderma edule were also present). A few Arenicola casts were visible. Macrofaunal content was mainly low numbers of Hediste diversicolor and occasional Cerastoderma edule together with other smaller worms. A 0.5m2 dig revealed the presence of two live Scrobicularia. Two cores 0.1m2/1mm mesh, and 0.01m2/0.5mm mesh were taken at 1m ACD. No infaunal data available. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

column3: Drift dive over sediment surface at 7m BCD. Rippled coarse sand sediment with a significant shell gravel content. The sediment showed many signs of burrowing activity with a prominent presence of Ensis ensis (siphons visible, also empty shells on surface), Bivalve siphons indet, Lanice conchylega (C), Terebellidae indet (large Lanice- like), Echinocardium cordatum (some empty casts on the surface). Occasional Cerianthus lloydii. Also Sagartiogeton undatus and Urticina felina were found as solitary individuals. Large numbers of drogonets Callionymus lyra were seen together with Gobidae indet (various sand gobies). Also large numbers of Asterias rubens and Carcinus maenas were seen on the surface. The surface was strewn with various sized mobile cobbles the larger ones being covered by Laminaria saccharina with attached hydroid and bryozoan growth. Smaller stones were largely colonised by Pomatoceros. Several fish species were noteably present especially Plaice and Pollack. Pleuronectes platessa was in the water column. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

column2: Steep very broken rock wit rockmills, crevices, steep slopes and horizontal tops with abundant Laminaria hyperborea and foliose algae undercover in places, Corallina sp was dominant but overall was common. Delesseria, Callophyllis and Cryptopleura were common on the tops and Phyllophora and Polyneura were common on the steep sides of the rock. Encrusting orange bryozoans were abundant on steep faces especially in rockmills. There was heavy encrusting fauna on many algae. Colonial ascidians were common, Botrylloides and Morchellium were most common and of the sponges Hymeniacidon was frequent. Alcyonium and Caryophillia were occasional. column2: Steep very broken rock with rockmills, crevices, steep slopes and horizontal tops with abundant Laminaria hyperborea and foliose algae undercover in places, Corallina sp was dominant but overall was common. Delesseria, Callophyllis and Cryptopleura were common on the tops and Phyllophora and Polyneura were common on the steep sides of the rock. Encrusting orange bryozoans were abundant on steep faces especially in rockmills. There was heavy encrusting fauna on many algae. Colonial ascidians were common, Botrylloides and Morchellium were most common and of the sponges Hymeniacidon was frequent. Alcyonium and Caryophillia were occasional. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.***

column2: Lower shore algae as substrate. column3: Middle shore broken bedrock with many rock ridges and small rockpools. Dominated by Porphyra with extensive cover, and Enteromorpha. A few fucoids (Fucus vesiculosus and F. serratus) in flatter areas, and patches of flat algal turf in damper areas and towards the lower middle shore. Barnacles were frequent (Chthamalus montagui, C. stellatus, Elminius modestus), with Semibalanus balanoides and Patella vulgata super abundant. Monodonta lineata, Actinia equina, Littorina menae and Hyale nilssoni were all frequent, and Gibbula umbilicalis occasional. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

column3: Sediment spot sample (1.8m ACD LMS). Cores 0.1m2/1mm and 0.01m2/0.5mm. Sediment was quite stable sandy mud with a shallow sink becoming firm at a few cm's depth although dark no black anoxic layer was found within the surface layers down to 15cm depth. Large quantities of detritus from terrestrial origin were retained in the sieves. The smooth surface was scantily covered with whole Mya shells and unattached Enteromorpha. Also large numbers of springtails were seen feeding on the surface layers. Some digging around the site revealed the presence of live Mya arenaria in the sediments whereas digging in the more coarse sediment areas downstream revealed no live specimens possibly indicating the less stable and coarse nature of the sediment being less habitable. There was a large number of Arenicola casts visible on the surface and some specimens were retained in the sieved cores. Also Hediste diversicolor was common together with smaller polycheate infauna. No infaunal data available. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

column2: The sediment comprised of coarse material largely shingle and gravel with interspersed grey silty mud moving into an anoxic blackening mixture at 5 to 10cm depth. column3: Coarse sandy mud with gravel, strongly rippled surface with very shallow sink. column2: Upper shore including splash zone. Lichens on steep bedrock, the usual upper shore Pelvetia/Bostrychia and F. spiralis/Catenella on flatter rock and more extensive bands. Enteromorpha was also abundant at this level, as were amphipods. Red mites were frequent, as well as Littorina saxitilis and L. littorea. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
column3: Silt slope with small stones from 0.5 to 1.5m BSL. Few foliose algae and Laminaria saccharina attached to stones. Gracilaria was frequent, occasional Sagartiogeton. Frequent hermit crabs and occasional Crangon. Sand gobies were frequent on the slope with occasional Balanus crenatus. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

column2: Pools at ca.3.8m ACD (partly in shade). Corallina officinalis was abundant. Enteromorpha, Ulva, Laminaria, Palmaria, rhodophyta indet and Grantia were all frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoirng system.
column3: Pools at ca.3.8m ACD (partly in shade). Corallina officinalis was abundant. Enteromorpha, Ulva, Laminaria, Palmaria, rhodophyta indet and Grantia were all frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

column2: Pools at ca. 5m ACD, in steeply sloping slate bedrock. Mytilus edulis, Enteromorpha and rhodophyta indet were all abundant. Actinia and Patella were both frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
column3: Scoured bedrock adjacent to sand and cobbles at 5m BCD. Bare rock (and rock dominated by encrusting Corallinaceae) here had been colonised by Audouinella sp and Ectocarpoidea with Laminaria saccharina sporelings and common Asterias rubens on the rock. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

column2: Splash zone above 4.8m ACD. Short zone ca 1m vertically with the usual zonation on steep and vertical bedrock. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
column3: Epiphytes on Zostera included abundant small Lacuna vincta, much Obelia geniculata and a few algal species. Leaves were generally clean except for Obelia.

column3: Walls of Penroll Bridge. Bridge stones colonised by common short F. ceranoides, Elminius modestus and Ulva. Hydrobia ulvae was frequent.

column2: Upper shore short zones of Pelvetia and Fucus spiralis from 4 to 4.8m, with Enteromorpha, Bostyrchia and Catenella both common and Hildenbrandia abundant. column2: Level seabed of stones on mud at ca 6m. Virtually bare of epibiota except scattered Metridium senile and included here only for the presence of one Calliactis parasitica on Pagurus cuanensis. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substrautm 1-3 scoring system.
column3: Level seabed of stones on mud at ca 6m. Virtually bare of epibiota except scattered Metridium senile and included here only for the presence of one Calliactis parasitica on Pagurus cuanensis. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

column2: Infralittoral rock spur at 0 to 2m BCD. Broken rock with extensive vertical, overhanging and upward facing rock dominated by tubiculous amphipods, with scattered foliose algae on upward facing surfaces and by Bugula turbinata and various hydroids on vertical surfaces. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
column3: Drift dive over sediment surface at 7m BCD. Rippled coarse sand sediment with a significant shell gravel content. The sediment showed many signs of burrowing activity with a prominent presence of Ensis ensis (siphons visible, also empty shells on surface), Bivalve siphons indet, Lanice conchylega (C), Terebellidae indet (large Lanice- like), Echinocardium cordatum (some empty casts on the surface). Occasional Cerianthus lloydii. Also Sagartiogeton undatus and Urticina felina were found as solitary individuals. Large numbers of drogonets Callionymus lyra were seen together with Gobidae indet (various sand gobies). Also large numbers of Asterias rubens and Carcinus maenas were seen on the surface. The surface was strewn with various sized mobile cobbles the larger ones being covered by Laminaria saccharina with attached hydroid and bryozoan growth. Smaller stones were largely colonised by Pomatoceros. Several fish species were noteably present especially Plaice and Pollack. Pleuronectes platessa was in the water column. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

column2: Upper shore at 5 to 6m ACD. Steeply sloping slate broken in places. Dominated by barnacles with common Patella vulgata and many littorinids. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
column3: Quartz pebbles and cobbles on mud. A steep slope 2m high with area at the top covered by pebbles and cobbles swarming with small Pagurus. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

column2: Walls of Pneroll Bridge. Bridge stones colonised by common short F. ceranoides, Elminius modestus and Ulva. Hydrobia ulvae was frequent.

column2: Upper shore bedrock. Dominated in turn by yellow lichens, (Xanthoria parietina, Caloplaca thallicola, Caloplaca maritima) then Verrucaria maura, then Pelvetia canaliculata and finally Fucus spiralis. Under Pelvetia and fucoids in shade were Audouinella sp and Catenella caespitosa and in less shade were encrusting dark red Rhodophyta indet. and less commonly Verrucaria mucosa. In pools under Fucus spiralis were Laomedea flexuosa and Clava multicornis. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
column3: Here the Laminaria hyperborea was frequent whilst Laminaria saccharina was common. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

column2: Lower shore bedrock, including overhangs. Large cover of Fucus serratus, with Ceramium rubrum becoming predominent at low water level (0.7m) and lesser abundance of a variety of small red and green algae Dynamena, Laomedea and Alcyonidium were again abundant on fucoids. Balanus crenatus was common and Elminius frequent, while Pomatoceros was common. Abundant amphipods and frequent Carcinus were present under fucoids. Bowerbankia imbricata was common and Hymeniacidon frequent. Occasional Asterias were present. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

column2: Middle shore broken bedrock with many rock ridges and small rockpools. Domtinated by Porphyra with extensive cover, and Enteromorpha. A few fucoids (Fucus vesiculosus and F. serratus) in flatter areas, and patches of flat algal turf in damper areas and towards the lower middle shore. Barnacles were frequent (Chthamalus montagui, C. stellatus, Elminius modestus), with Semibalanus balanoides and Patella vulgata super abundant. Monodonta lineata, Actinia equina, Littorina menae and Hyale nilssoni were all frequent, and Gibbula umbilicalis occasional. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.

column2: Very coarse sediment with broken slates and assorted stones, small cobbles and pebbles at 10.2 m bcd. The sediment contained many polychaetes with a very large count of Apseudes latreillii. Encrusting growth was found on the slates accounting for the presence of Pomatoceros lamarckii, Verruca stroemia, Dynamena pumila and dwarf Metridium senile. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
column3: Here the Laminaria hyperborea was frequent whilst Laminaria saccharina was common. Encrusting dark red algae were abundant and pink encrusting algae were common, Ahnfeltia, Phyllophora, Polyneura, Callophyllis and Phyllophora were frequent. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
